data.title: Robinhood Stock Jumps as Uniswap Deal Boosts Crypto Access
data.description:
Robinhood (HOOD) stock surged 2.4% at the start of regular trading hours today as investors reacted to its latest moveâpartnering with Uniswap, MoonPay, and Transak to simplify crypto-to-cash conversions.

Uniswapâs New Partnership Makes Crypto Cashouts Seamless

Uniswap, the worldâs largest decentralized exchange, has teamed up with Robinhood, MoonPay, and Transak to let users sell their crypto directly for fiat. According to Uniswapâs announcement, this new feature is now live in over 180 countries through Uniswapâs mobile wallet and will soon be available on its web app and browser extension.

SEC Closes Investigations into Uniswap and Robinhood

The new partnership comes just days after the SEC dropped its investigations into both Uniswap and Robinhoodâs crypto division. The agency had been scrutinizing Robinhoodâs crypto operations but ultimately decided not to take enforcement action. Similarly, Uniswap had been under regulatory pressure since receiving a Wells notice in April 2023. With these legal uncertainties lifted, both companies now have room to expand their crypto services without fear of immediate regulatory roadblocks.

UNI Token Drops Despite Positive News

While Robinhood stock saw a boost, Uniswapâs UNI (UNI-USD) token fell 5.4% to $7.60, weighed down by a broader crypto market slump. However, the long-term impact of this partnership could strengthen Uniswapâs position in decentralized finance.

Is HOOD Stock a Good Buy?

Analysts are cautiously bullish about HOOD stock, with a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 12 Buys and 5 Holds. Over the past year, HOOD has increased byÂ more than 210%, and theÂ average HOOD price targetÂ of $71.27 implies an upside potential of 42.6% from current levels.

See more HOOD analyst ratings
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Apple (AAPL) Moves to Facial Recognition with New iPhone Model
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is moving to facial recognition with its new more affordable iPhone model.

The iPhone 16e, which begins shipping today (Feb. 28), is adding face identification as its main biometric security feature, replacing the fingerprint technology or “Touch ID” that has long been the primary way for users to access the smartphones.

Appleâs new iPhone is aimed at more budget-conscious consumers and is the first smartphone from the consumer electronics giant to focus on Face ID to unlock the device rather than a fingerprint scan.

âItâs the most effortless way of authenticating,â said Joe Palmer, chief innovation officer at iProov, a global technology company focused on biometric verification and authentication. If you think about how many times you unlock a phone in a day, even if it takes you a second and youâre unlocking the phone 100 times a day, it adds up, he said. âI donât think weâre going to see an evolution beyond face anytime soon,â he added.

Still, technology and cybersecurity professionals say fingerprint scanning technology has plenty of life left â and Apple itself is likely to offer the option in future device releases, including smartphones.

Hereâs what consumers need to understand about the latest biometric trends in smartphones, and whatâs likely to come next:

Why fingerprints could still make a comeback
Appleâs Touch ID continues to be available in certain iPad models, and the company is likely to reintroduce the technology in subsequent versions of its smartphones, according to experts consulted by CNBC. One sign they point to that makes this likely: The company was granted a patent several years ago for under-display fingerprint reading technology and continues to work on improvements, according to several published reports. As a result, the company is likely to bring back Touch ID to smartphones once it perfects its version of under-the-screen technology.

Apple declined to comment.

Consumers like choices, Palmer said, offering the example of a colleague who uses facial authentication to unlock an Android phone and fingerprint technology to authorize payments. Once Apple introduces fingerprint technology under the screen, it will likely be available in flagship phones again and work its way down through the models, he said.

Appleâs near-term move away from fingerprint technology in its smartphones makes sense for several reasons. For one, the company has always had a larger facial recognition culture, in part because its technology is solid and easy to use, said Roger Grimes, an analyst at KnowBe4, a security platform provider.

Itâs designed to automatically adapt to changes in user appearance, such as wearing cosmetic makeup or growing facial hair. Itâs also designed to work with hats, scarves, glasses, contact lenses and many sunglasses. The company designed the technology to work indoors, outdoors, and even in total darkness. With iPhone 12 or later, Face ID also works with face masks.

The move away from Touch ID on smartphones is also an attempt to appeal to customers who want more screen space on their devices, technology professionals said. In past phone versions, Appleâs Touch ID fingerprint sensor was integrated into a phoneâs home or power button. Whereas the iPhone 16e â similar to the iPhone 10 â has a notch, a physical area on its display for sensors. This design element has been used in smartphones for several years to accommodate front-facing cameras and microphones while meeting consumer demand for larger edge-to-edge screens. âApple has been slowly trying to remove the home button from phones for many years to get the edge-to-edge experience where the entire phone is a screen and thereâs no wasted space,â Palmer said.

Thumb tech is cost-effective
Fingerprint technology continues to be available on Android devices, and thatâs not likely to change anytime soon, even as newer phones offer facial authentication as an option, said Jean Fang, senior consultant for biometrics and authentication at Fime, which offers consulting and testing services to the payments industry.

Face Unlock is available on Pixel 4 and Pixel 7 or later Pixel phones, including Pixel Fold, according to Googleâs website. On Pixel 8 and later, consumers can use Face Unlock to verify their identity when they sign into apps or approve a purchase. The face recognition feature can be used on Galaxy phones or tablets to unlock the device and verify the userâs identity in certain apps, according to Samsungâs website.

Even as more devices adopt facial authentication, fingerprint technology will remain a solid option for many phone users, technology professionals said. For one thing, fingerprint scanning is more cost-effective than other options such as iris or palm scans. âItâs a very good technology and itâs very mature and we have fingerprint sensors that are affordable everywhere,â said D. J. Lee, a professor in the department of electrical and computer engineering at Brigham Young University.

âIt works the way we need it to work most of the time,â said Grimes.

Biometric security limitations
To be sure, there are downsides to popular biometric options. Fingerprint authentication doesnât always work properly, if, for example, a personâs finger is wet or chapped, or the sensor canât detect an exact match for another reason. But facial authentication technology also has drawbacks, especially as deepfake technology advances, said Fang, who is also a member of the Secure Technology Alliance, a not-for-profit, multi-industry association focused on identity, access and payments. There can also be limitations on how well facial authentication works depending on factors such as lighting and whether the person had facial surgery such as a nose job or eyebrow lift, she said.

âIt can be a good feature for some lower-risk cases, but not all cases,â Fang said.

Despite the limitations of existing biometric modules, fingerprint and face authentication technology are expected to be the go-to biometric methods for the foreseeable future. Thatâs not for lack of testing of other methods, but for more practical reasons. About 15 years ago, Grimes participated in a product test that tried to identify users by smell, which seemed to work well until the test subjects ate a lot of garlic or drank alcohol. âIt turned out a lot of people really liked garlic and that would overwhelm their scent and you have a lot of people that drink a lot,â he said.

While itâs possible to authenticate users through other biometric methods, like iris or palm scans â Amazon Whole Foodsâ stores palm payments tech being a recent example â in many cases these may cost more and add more friction for users, making widespread adoption less likely. âItâs the balance between security, the convenience and the cost,â Lee said.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Apple has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 33 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 18 Buy, 11 Hold, and four Sell recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $250.95 implies 1.02% upside from current levels.

Read more analyst ratings on AAPL stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Trump’s Tax-Free Crypto Plan Could Backfire and Wreak Havoc
data.description:
Trumpâs rumored plan to eliminate capital gains tax on U.S.-based cryptocurrencies might sound like a win for investors, but experts warn it could destabilize global crypto markets.

Trump’s Plan Could Happen Immediately

If Trumpâs administration follows through on the reported tax exemption, U.S. investors could dump non-U.S. cryptos to rotate into tax-free domestic options. This shift could increase sell pressure on global projects, particularly those heavily reliant on U.S. investors. As cited by CoinDesk, analysts warn that this sudden change could trigger price volatility across the board.

A Flood of New Coins Could Cause Scams

Zero capital gains tax might fuel a crypto gold rush in the U.S., much like the 2017 ICO boom, which saw nearly 80% of projects collapse or turn into scams. If the tax break arrives before clear regulations, bad actors could take advantage of inexperienced investors, damaging cryptoâs reputation. The FBI has already warned about fake tokens preying on unsuspecting buyers.

Could This Hurt the Global Crypto Industry?

With U.S. venture capital firms favoring local tokens to maximize tax-free gains, global projects may struggle to secure funding. Countries like the UAE have tax-free crypto policies, but they apply them universallyâunlike Trumpâs proposed U.S.-only plan. According to CoinDesk, this could fragment liquidity, reduce competition, and isolate American investors from the broader crypto economy. It’s also important to note that while tax relief may sound attractive, it risks fueling scams, weakening global markets, and stifling innovation.

It is important to see how these developments impact crypto prices. Therefore, investors should stay ahead by tracking well-established cryptos on TipRanksâ Cryptocurrency Center. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: XRP Whales Buy the Dip as Short-Term Holders Sell Off
data.description:
Short-term XRP (XRP-USD) holders are offloading their coins, but whales are seizing the moment to accumulate billions in tokens. Could this signal a price turnaround?

Whales Absorb Selling Pressure from Panic Sellers

XRPâs price has struggled in recent days, dipping below $2.12 as the broader crypto market faces pressure. According to CoinGlass, short-term holders have been the primary sellers, rushing to exit after the latest market crash. Data from Santiment shows that the majority of coins sold were held for less than 180 days, with many investors cutting their losses.

However, while small traders panic, XRP whales are doing the opposite. Large holdersâwallets with 1M-100M XRPâhave accumulated nearly 440 million tokens, worth over $1 billion, since the crash began. As noted by CryptoQuant, this kind of accumulation often precedes price recoveries.

Will XRP Hold Support or Drop Further?

XRPâs price is now hovering near a crucial support level at $1.96. Analysts warn that if this level fails, XRP could slide further toward the lower boundary of a descending channel. However, if buying pressure from whales continues, a bounce could follow.

Technical indicators remain mixed. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) suggests that XRP is in oversold territory, which could mean a rebound is coming. Meanwhile, Rippleâs expansion into South Korea with BDACS aims to strengthen institutional demand, which could support long-term growth.

It seems like XRP’s next price moves could depend on the battle between short-term sellers and deep-pocketed whales. At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $2.1161.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: CME Group Unleashes Solana Futures as Crypto Derivatives Surge
data.description:
CME Group (CME) is doubling down on crypto derivatives. The worldâs largest derivatives marketplace announced it will launch Solana (SOL-USD) futures on March 17, pending regulatory approval. This move adds another major asset to its crypto suite, which already includes Bitcoin and Ethereum futures.

“With the launch of our new SOL futures contracts, we are responding to increasing client demand for a broader set of regulated products to manage cryptocurrency price risk,” said Giovanni Vicioso, CME Groupâs Global Head of Cryptocurrency Products.

Solana Futures Offer Institutional Trading Tools

The new Solana futures will be cash-settled and based on the CME CF Solana-Dollar Reference Rate, which tracks SOLâs price daily at 4:00 p.m. London time. Traders will have two contract size options: a smaller 25 SOL contract and a larger 500 SOL contract.

This is a big deal for institutional investors. “The launch of SOL futures is a significant milestone in the ongoing maturation of the cryptocurrency market,” said Teddy Fusaro, President of Bitwise Asset Management. He added that CME Group has been instrumental in legitimizing crypto as an asset class through its regulated financial products.

Rising Demand Fuels CMEâs Crypto Expansion

CMEâs decision to introduce SOL futures comes amid explosive growth in crypto derivatives trading. The firm reported an average daily volume of 202,000 contracts this year, up 73% from 2024. Open interest has also jumped 55% year-over-year, with over 11,300 unique accounts trading.

According to Kyle Samani, Co-Founder and Managing Partner at Multicoin Capital, “As the digital asset market matures and demand continues to grow, sophisticated investors need better ways to get exposure and manage volatility. CME Groupâs new crypto derivatives provide just that.”

Retail and Institutional Traders Gain New Opportunities

Itâs not just institutions that stand to benefit. “The new SOL futures contracts are further examples of CME Group innovating in the retail crypto trading space for the benefit of customers,” said Elad Even-Chen, Group CFO of Plus500. These contracts will give retail traders more flexibility in managing their portfolios with diversified exposure to crypto assets.

As a result of the news, SOL was up 4.22% today. At the time of writing, SOL is sitting at $143.41.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Risk of Plunge as $75K Support Teeters
data.description:
Bitcoin is skating on thin ice as it hovers around the critical $75,000 support level. A deeper correction is on the table, and analysts warn that if this level breaks, the cryptocurrency could slide furtherâpossibly toward $70,000, according to Cointelegraph. Bitcoin has already dropped over 6.5% in 24 hours, sinking below $78,197 for the first time since November 2024.

Macroeconomic fears fuel Bitcoinâs sell-off

Investors are rattled by mounting global trade concerns, particularly the potential trade war between the U.S. and China. The latest catalyst? U.S. President Donald Trumpâs import tariffs, which have intensified fears of economic instability. “Bitcoinâs drop below $80,000 amid investor fear from Trumpâs tariffs and market unrest points to a correction likely hitting $76,000-$78,000 this week,” said Ryan Lee, chief analyst at Bitget Research, in a statement to Cointelegraph.

Will Bitcoin Break Below $75K?

The $75,000 level is now the line in the sand. If Bitcoin drops below it, nearly $900 million in leveraged long liquidations could be triggered across exchanges, according to CoinGlass data. While some analysts suggest Bitcoin could hold above $75,000 due to institutional dip buying, Lee cautioned that a drop to $70,000 is still possible if market sentiment worsens: “A further plunge to $70,000 is possible but less probable by March 2 without a significant new shock.”

Historical Patterns Hint at More Downside Ahead

Bitcoinâs correction isnât out of the ordinary. Crypto analyst Rekt Capital pointed out that the current -25% pullback is in line with historical trends. “Depth-wise, this current -25% Price Discovery Correction has been shallower by standards of history though still quite close to the -30% mark,” he wrote in a Feb. 27 post on X. If past cycles repeat, Bitcoin could face two more weeks of downward pressure before finding stability.

Can Bitcoin Defy the Odds?

Despite the grim outlook, some believe a sharp plunge to $70,000 isnât imminent. With big players like Michael Saylorâs Strategy (previously known as MicroStrategy) (MSTR) continuing to accumulate Bitcoin, dip-buying from institutions might provide a buffer.

However, unless Bitcoin holds $75,000 convincingly, the coming days could be a test of nerves for investors. At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $81,784.27.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: SEC Case May Near End as Ripple Fights for Freedom
data.description:
Rippleâs long battle with the SEC may be nearing its end. A potential settlement could boost XRPâs (XRP-USD) legitimacy and set the stage for a price rebound.

SEC’s Lawsuit Against Ripple Could Be Dropped

After more than four years of legal battles, the SEC vs. Ripple case may finally be approaching a resolution. According to Cointelegraph, legal experts are speculating that both sides could agree to drop their appeals, with Ripple potentially paying a $125 million settlement. XRP investors have been closely watching recent SEC settlements with Coinbase (COIN) and Robinhood (HOOD), hoping Ripple could see a similar outcome.

Fred Rispoli, a pro-XRP attorney, suggested that the case “could end any day now” if both parties agree to move forward. However, others, including MetaLawMan, believe the SEC might delay the case to align with other lawsuits against major exchanges like Binance and Kraken.

Could This Be XRPâs Turning Point?

If Ripple secures a favorable outcome, the impact on XRP could be significant. A dismissal of the case would not only remove regulatory uncertainty but could also lead to greater institutional adoption. According to CoinDesk, some speculate that a lawsuit resolution could even pave the way for an XRP exchange-traded fund (ETF).

For now, XRP fans hope that if the agency backs down, Ripple could finally regain its footing. XRP might then see a long-awaited price rally.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Bitcoin Plummets 30% as “Trump Pump” Gets Wiped Out
data.description:
Bitcoinâs price has plummeted nearly 30% from its all-time high, wiping out the so-called âTrump pump.â As reported by Cointelegraph, traders have been closely watching a significant CME futures gap that formed during Bitcoinâs surge past $100,000. That gap, located around $78,000, is now nearly filled. Historically, CME gaps tend to act as price magnets, pulling Bitcoin back to levels where trading activity had previously been absent.

Crypto analyst Rekt Capital confirmed on X that Bitcoin is âgetting closer and closer to filling its CME gap formed back in November 2024.â If this gap is fully closed, some traders anticipate a relief bounce, potentially sending BTC back toward the $92,000 region, where another CME gap remains open.

Whales Stack Up Bids at Lower Levels

Despite the panic, large traders appear to be taking advantage of the dip. According to Daan Crypto Trades, around $1.8 billion in Bitcoin buy orders have emerged between $70,000 and $79,000 on Binance futures. While these bids could be pulled at any moment, they suggest that institutional investors see value at these lower levels.

Meanwhile, blockchain analytics firm Glassnode reports that over $3 billion in long positions have been liquidated in the past five days. This rapid unwinding of leveraged positions has exacerbated Bitcoinâs decline, making it a textbook case of a âfalling knife.â

Is This Just a Retest Before the Next Rally?

While many are fearful, some analysts see Bitcoinâs current dip as a classic âbreakout and retestâ play. As noted by CoinDesk, markets often revisit former resistance levels to confirm them as new support before resuming their upward trajectory. Bitcoinâs previous resistance at $73,757âbroken in Novemberâcould now serve as a crucial support zone.

If Bitcoin holds these levels, history suggests a potential rebound. In both 2020 and 2023, similar retests led to strong rallies that pushed Bitcoin to new all-time highs. However, if support fails, a deeper correction could unfold.

Market Sentiment Remains Fragile

While technical indicators suggest a possible bottom, broader market sentiment remains shaky. The Kobeissi Letter points out that Bitcoin has shown increased correlation with traditional stocks, which have been under pressure due to concerns over new U.S. tariffs on Canada and Mexico. The resulting economic uncertainty has driven investors toward the U.S. dollar. As a result, this has naturally weakened demand for risk assets like Bitcoin.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $80,225.98.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: SoundHound AI Stock Gains Over 6%, Let’s Look at Who Owns SOUN
data.description:
Voice AI software company SoundHound AI (SOUN) stock is up over 6% in the pre-market trading after the company reported strong Q4 earnings. EPS came in at -$0.05, beating analysts’ estimate of -$0.10. Meanwhile, revenue rose 101% year-over-year to $34.5 million. CEO Keyvan Mohajer credited the growth to major customer wins and expanding partnerships, including deals with Burger King UK and Lucid Motors (LCID). Given these developments, itâs the right time to delve into the ownership structure of SOUN.

Now, according toÂ TipRanksâ ownership page, public companies and individual investors own 69.83% of MU. They are followed by ETFs, mutual funds, other institutional investors, and insiders at 15.11%, 8.85%, and 4.07%, and 2.14%, respectively.

Digging Deeper into SOUNâs Ownership Structure

Looking closely at top shareholders, Vanguard owns the highest stake in SOUN at 8.41%. Next up is Vanguard Index Funds, which holds a 3.99% stake in the company.

Among the top ETF holders, Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI) owns a 2.79% stake in Boeing stock, followed by iShares Russell 2000 ETF (IWM) with a 2.30% stake.

Moving to Mutual Funds, Vanguard Index Funds holds about 3.99% of SOUN, whereas Vanguard World Fund owns 1.77% of the stock.

Is SoundHound Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SOUN stock based on 2 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. TheÂ average SOUN price target of $16.50 per shareÂ implies 79.15% upside potential.

Conclusion

TipRanksâ Ownership Tab provides valuable insights into the category-wise ownership structure of the company, enabling investors to make well-informed investment decisions.

data.title: Barclays Hikes the Price Target on JD.com Stock Ahead of Q4 Results
data.description:
JD.com (JD)Â stock forecast received a boost from Barclaysâ price target hike ahead of its Q4 results. AnalystÂ Gregory Zhao of BarclaysÂ lifted the price target on JD stock to $55 from $50 yesterday, while maintaining his âBuyâ rating. The revised price target implies an impressive 29.3% upside potential from current levels.

Meanwhile, JD stock has surged more than 91% over the past year, driven by cost savings, improving consumer sentiment, and operational efficiency. Additionally, the company’s partnerships with other brands have contributed to its growth in the market.

Zhao’s Views on JD Stock

Zhao expects JD to deliver around 10% year-over-year revenue growth in Q4, supported by strong profit margins. He believes that the Chinese government’s economic stimulus measures will play a major role in driving growth through 2025, positioning JD to benefit significantly. With these positive factors in mind, Zhao increased his estimates, leading to the higher price target and reaffirming his optimistic outlook on the stock.

Bank of America Stays Bullish on JD With $48 PT

Similarly, Bank of America analyst Joyce Ju also maintained a “Buy” rating on JD.com with a $48 price target ahead of Q4 results. Ju expects strong Q4 2024 results, supported by government subsidies and promotions. She forecasts 10.1% year-over-year revenue growth, driven by trade-in programs and Singles Day sales.She believes ongoing government stimulus will continue to drive growth in JD’s core electronics and home appliances businesses through 2025.

Whatâs Next for JD Stock?

JD will report its Q4 results next week on March 6. The Q4 2024 results are likely to reflect the gains from the Chinese government’s stimulus, boosting sales in a few categories, including home appliances and electronics.

Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings of $0.85 per share, representing a 17% increase year-over-year. At the same time, revenues are expected to grow by 7% from the year-ago quarter to $45.4 billion, according to data from the TipRanks Forecast page. Notably, it has consistently beaten EPS estimates for the past nine quarters.

Is JD.com Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on JD stock based on eight Buys and one Hold recommendation assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. TheÂ average JD stock price targetÂ is $50.13, implying an upside potential of 17.84%.

See more JD analyst ratings
